title: tanacetum myconis
side_effects: Tanacetum myconis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extract can lead to symptoms like dizziness, nausea, and headaches. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oil can also cause respiratory problems, including coughing and shortness of breath. Furthermore, individuals with pre-existing medical conditions, such as kidney or liver disease, should exercise extreme caution when handling Tanacetum myconis, as it may exacerbate their condition.
